pattern: Recovering the first element via the XOR of all values and the XOR of odd-indexed encoded entries lets the rest unfold by prefix XOR.
hints: The XOR of the whole permutation is known because it is a permutation of 1..n.
XOR of perm[1],perm[2],... can be obtained from the odd-positioned encoded values, isolating perm[0].
total = XOR of 1..n; odd = encoded[1]^encoded[3]^...; perm[0] = total ^ odd; then perm[i] = perm[i-1] ^ encoded[i-1].

stress.description: build a random odd-length permutation then encode it as adjacent XORs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 99999))
    if n % 2 == 0:
        n += 1
    perm = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(perm)
    encoded = [perm[i] ^ perm[i + 1] for i in range(n - 1)]
    return [encoded]
